name: AI Scene
description: Full-bleed AI-generated image whose prompt is rewritten every refresh from live data: weather, calendar events, Home Assistant entities, time of day. The image adapts to the dashboard's actual context. Bring your own Fal.ai API key (configured once on the AI Core plugin). Defaults to Flux Schnell (~$0.003/image, ~$2.20/month at hourly refresh).

cell_options.name: prompt_template
cell_options.label: Prompt template
cell_options.default: A serene watercolor of {weather.now.condition} at {time.am_pm}. Soft natural light, painterly brushstrokes, no text, no people. Mood: calm.
cell_options.help: Click a chip below to insert a placeholder at the cursor. The resolved prompt is sent to Fal.ai; missing data renders as "unknown" so the image always generates.

cell_options.name: latitude
cell_options.label: Latitude (for weather)
cell_options.help: Leave empty to use the server-level home location (Settings → Server → Location).

cell_options.name: longitude
cell_options.label: Longitude (for weather)
cell_options.help: Leave empty to use the server-level home location.

cell_options.name: ha_entities
cell_options.label: Home Assistant entities whitelist
cell_options.help: Comma-separated entity IDs that {ha.entity.<entity_id>.state} is allowed to read. Empty = no HA placeholders resolve.

cell_options.name: negative_prompt
cell_options.label: Negative prompt (optional)
cell_options.default: blurry, low quality, watermark, signature, text, logo, frame
cell_options.help: What you don't want in the image. Ignored by some models (Flux Schnell).

cell_options.name: refresh_minutes
cell_options.label: Refresh interval (minutes)
cell_options.help: Hourly refresh on Flux Schnell costs roughly $2.20/month. Cached frames are reused until the resolved prompt actually changes.

cell_options.name: image_width
cell_options.label: Fal request width (px)
cell_options.help: Leave empty to use the cell's actual width (rounded up to the next multiple of 64, clamped to [512, 2048]). Set explicitly to override. Note: Nano Banana and Nano Banana 2 ignore custom dimensions and always render 1024×1024 — pick a different model if you need a non-square output.

cell_options.name: image_height
cell_options.label: Fal request height (px)
cell_options.help: Leave empty to use the cell's actual height (same rounding rules as the width).
